data.title: Pump.fun Launches a $10M Funding Program. Is This Another "Rug Pull"?
data.description:
Pump.fun steps out of meme chaos to back real startups across crypto and beyond

Pump.fun, the Solana-centered platform behind countless viral memecoins, has just launched Pump Fund, a $3 million investment fund designed to throw in a whopping $10 million dollars to support budding projects. The fund is currently aiming to reach out to 12 startups, with each project getting a fat check of $250,000.

Introducing the $3,000,000 Build in Public Hackathon

Brought to you by Pump Fund - pump fun’s New Investment Arm

It’s time to completely reimagine how early-stage projects are built and funded.

Learn more 👇 pic.twitter.com/l1TJcxv1J0

— Pump.fun (@Pumpfun) January 19, 2026

This new project comes just one week after Pump.fun set records for Solana memecoin activity. The initiative marks a strategic shift from speculative token launches toward fostering broader innovation across the crypto and non-crypto sectors.

The fund is open to projects at all maturity stages and across all industries, with no requirement to be blockchain-related. Selection criteria focus on execution speed, transparency, product or social traction, and long-term viability.

Beyond capital injection, Pump.Fun has also promised mentor selected teams, granting them direct mentorship from Pump.Fun's founders, reinforcing the program as a comprehensive support system rather than a simple funding initiative.

For startups, Pump.Fun promises fast and flexible financing combined with exposure to a high-profile network of experts. By reaching out to projects outside of crypto, it also essentiates Pump.Fun's desire to extend diversity, a trend to follow for other sector players.

🔥 @Pumpfun unveils a new investment arm to back emerging startups#Pumpfun has introduced #PumpFund, a new investment arm focused on supporting startups within its ecosystem. Its first move is a Build in Public Hackathon that will allocate $3M in funding to 12 projects. To… pic.twitter.com/Hirf5667Pi

— PHOENIX - Crypto News & Analytics (@pnxgrp) January 20, 2026

While Pump Fund could enhance Pump.fun’s credibility among serious entrepreneurs, its infamous reputation for memecoins and "Rug Pulls" right pose credibility challenges with traditional investors.

It remains to be seen if Pump.Fund will identify and support high-impact projects, and how this strategy will influence the crypto ecosystem in the long term.

Funding startups could drive adoption and strengthen Solana’s ecosystem

Pump.fun’s deep integration with the Solana network means that the blockchain could benefit substantially from the expansion. Startups funded by Pump Fund may build applications, protocols, or consumer-facing products on Solana, generating new transaction volume and gas fee demand, which could strengthen the network’s adoption and credibility.

By attracting innovative projects to Solana, the platform not only diversifies its portfolio but also potentially boosts SOL token usage and encourages further ecosystem growth. The success of Pump Fund could reinforce Solana’s position as a startup-friendly blockchain, aligning the platform’s financial incentives with broader ecosystem expansion.

The initiative also demonstrates Pump.fun’s long-term vision: moving away from meme-centric hype and toward responsible capital allocation and strategic diversification, with the dual aim of supporting startups and enhancing network utility.

Yet, with high stakes and lingering credibility concerns, whether Pump Fund will fully deliver on these promises remains to be seen.

data.title: Deconstructing the growth spiral and volume of Meme using first principles
data.description:
Author: danny; Source: X, @agintender

In most people's eyes, Meme coin is an irrational frenzy, a gambling game of the greater fool theory. But from a mathematical perspective, the birth of every 10,000x myth is not accidental; it's actually a middle school spatial geometry application problem.

This article proposes a disruptive perspective: Meme coin's market capitalization is not "rising," but rather "inflated." Meme's value can be calculated!

We are used to focusing on the fluctuations of the Z-axis (price height), but we ignore the fact that what determines life and death is the radius of the base constructed by X (narrative density) and Y (propagation nodes).

A meme with only height and no base is merely a thin needle, easily blown over by the wind; only an ever-expanding radius of consensus can, under the gravity of capital, support a stable, geometrically growing cone of wealth. Note: This article is a popular science piece on "financial physics," not a rigorous econometrics paper, simply offering a fresh perspective on memes. 1. The Beginning of the Theory: The Three-Dimensional Coordinate System of Memes In the current cryptocurrency market, meme coins are often seen as an irrational frenzy. But is that really the case? If we strip away emotional noise, KOL hype, and community hype, we find that the birth and demise of every phenomenal meme follows a rigorous mathematical logic. The essence of meme coins is the tokenization of the attention economy. Their market capitalization is not determined by traditional discounted cash flow (DCF), but rather by the breadth of the narrative, the resonance of the community, and the explosive power of funding. For better discussion, we define these factors as the XYZ three-dimensional growth spiral model. These three axes are not merely independent variables; they exhibit strong reflexivity—a change in one variable reinforces another, forming a positive feedback loop. Let's explain these parameters: X-axis: Narrative density and cultural memes. Definition: The "genes" of a meme. This includes core memes (such as Doge), origin stories (such as CZ's pet name), cultural symbols (such as Pepe's Sad Frog), and the richness of community-created content. Key metrics: Narrative originality, replicability, and emotional resonance. Y-axis: Dissemination potential and node network. Definition: The information transmission channel. From top-level nodes (CZ, Elon Musk) to secondary nodes (Alpha Callers, KOLs), and then to end-level nodes (ordinary retail investors). Key metrics: Node weight, dissemination coverage, and frequency of recommendations. Z-axis: Fund flow and liquidity carrier. Definition: The monetization of attention. Including on-chain fund inflows and liquidity depth—that is, the value held or the total amount of sell orders that can be absorbed. Key metrics: market capitalization, trading volume, turnover rate, and liquidity (the most important metric). 2. A diagram illustrating the growth curve of the interaction between the XYZ axes. If we plot these three axes in three-dimensional space, a successful meme growth trajectory generally follows a spiral upward trend: (as shown in the diagram below). Start: X, Y, and Z are all close to zero. Ignition: A sudden increase in the Y-axis (KOL recommendations) drives a jump in the Z-axis (price). The trajectory line tilts towards the Y-Z plane. Spin: The growth of the Z-axis attracts more Y (more discussion), and Y inspires secondary creation in X (richer content). The trajectory line begins to rotate around the X-Y-Z axes. Ascension: As X is upgraded (X'), the base area of ​​the cone expands (the consensus range widens), and the Z-axis (market capitalization) reaches a new height.

Failed Meme (Rug Pull): Typically manifested as a surge on the Y-axis followed by a brief rise on the Z-axis, but the X-axis fails to follow suit (lack of narrative), causing the Z-axis to quickly collapse to zero, resulting in a V-shaped drop in the trajectory.

3. Evolution of the Growth Curve: A Spiral Ascent in Four Stages

From the previous section, we know that the life cycle of a meme is not linear, but rather a vortex that continuously expands around the Z-axis (fund flow). In this section, we will analyze it based on the four development stages of a meme.

The following diagram shows its standard evolutionary path: (Note: For ease of reading and understanding, I will not write it in paragraphs.) **Phase 1: Ignition (The Ignition) — X + Y → ΔZ** A powerful narrative (X) combined with a high-weight propagation node (Y), instantly triggering a surge in funds (Z).

  • Taking Broccoli as an example:

  • X (narrative): On February 13, 2025, CZ announced that his pet dog's name was "Broccoli". This is not just a simple pet name; it carries a deeper metaphor of "CZ's comeback" and "BNB's ecosystem return." Furthermore, "Broccoli" itself possesses a unique sound signature and visual image (green = rising). Y (Node): CZ himself, as a top-tier traffic node in the industry (Y-Max), had a tweet with a propagation potential exceeding even a Super Bowl ad. Z (Result): Instantaneous capital response. Hundreds of tokens with the same name quickly emerged on Solana and BNB Chain, with the top token's market capitalization exceeding $1.5 billion within hours, and early investors profiting millions within 20 minutes. The growth on the Z-axis in this stage is pulse-like, entirely dependent on a single-point burst on the Y-axis. Second Stage: Establishment of Reflexivity (The Reflexivity Loop) — Z↑→ Y↑

    • Price (Z) itself becomes the best advertising and dissemination channel (Y).

    • When the Z-axis (market capitalization) rises rapidly, creating a "hundred-fold golden dog" wealth effect, it will have a reverse effect on the Y-axis.

    • Passive dissemination: KOLs who originally did not pay attention to this meme start to actively mention it in order to ride the wave (traffic capture).

    • FOMO diffusion: The top gainers list itself is the strongest dissemination node. Media reports (such as "a certain address profited $6 million") further expand the dissemination radius.

    • BNB Chain's strategy: BNB Chain officially promotes the meme through the "Meme Innovation Competition" and the "Daily Airdrop" program (daily purchases) The 33 BNB "golden dog" airdropped to the community artificially injected energy into the Z-axis to maintain the activity of the Y-axis. The expanded propagation network (Y) forces the narrative (X) to undergo a second iteration (X'). Simply relying on the "CZ dog" meme (X) cannot support long-term market capitalization. When the Y-axis expands to hundreds of thousands of holding addresses, the community begins to spontaneously create new narratives (X'). From "meme" to "culture": the community begins to create memes, establish a "broccoli cult," and imbue the token with a certain spiritual sustenance (such as "resisting VC coins," "BNB Chain"). "A symbol of revival"). Instrumentalized narrative: such as FLOKI evolving from a simple meme into an ecosystem with Valhalla games and DeFi protocols. This is also an X → X' upgrade. The fourth stage: The Value Black Hole – X' → Z_max. The upgraded narrative (X') can support a larger scale of funds (Z). When the X-axis completes the upgrade from "monster" to "totem," the upper limit of the Z-axis is opened. left;">Change in the nature of funds: From early "quick in, quick out" smart money to "diamond hand" community funds and institutional funds attempting allocation.

    • Infrastructure leeching: At this point, Meme is no longer just a customer acquisition tool; it begins to feed back into the on-chain infrastructure. The huge transaction volume brings BNB Chain a large amount of BNB burning and fee revenue, while also providing liquidity entry points for other applications within the ecosystem (such as Aster derivatives trading and Pancake trading volume).

    4. A conjecture sparked by a cone formula?

    Attentive readers may notice that the 3D diagram above resembles a cone. Is it possible that we can find the volume formula for this cone?

    V = ⅓ * base area * height This formula is applied to the growth model of Meme coin

    4.1. Variable Mapping: Constructing the Growth Model

    We map the various geometric elements of the cone to the variables x, y, z you proposed:

    Base radius r = x * y : Boundary of social consensus

    • x (content/narrative): This is Meme The "genes" of consensus. Good content determines the contagiousness of consensus. y (propagation nodes): These are the community, KOLs, and token holders. Nodes determine the coverage of the narrative. r (radius): Represents the physical boundary of consensus. The larger the radius, the more people know and are willing to believe the story, and the more stable the foundation. height h = z (funding/emotional leverage): The speed and density of fund inflows determine the vertical height of price increases. Volume V = Market Cap: The final market capitalization. 4.1.1 Why is the radius x * y a square relationship? In Meme In the world of Meme, the combination of narrative and nodes has a network effect. If content x is merely a mediocre imitation, even with numerous nodes y, the radius r is difficult to expand. When a highly penetrating narrative (x) encounters frequently interacting propagation nodes (y), the consensus area expands exponentially. The squared term in the formula means that a slight expansion of the underlying community consensus will result in a squared increase in its supported volume (market capitalization) potential. 4.1.2 Why is the capital z linear in height? Capital z determines how high the price can go. However, in the Meme model, a capital height without a base area (x and y) is unstable. If there is only z (whale entry to pump the price) but no r (broad retail investor consensus), the cone will become an extremely thin "needle," collapsing at the slightest disturbance. True blue-chip memes (such as DOGE and PEPE) have a huge base diameter, which makes their overall volume still enormous even with high capital fluctuations. Scientists have discovered that the volume of a cone is only one-third that of a cylinder with the same base and height. In the meme market, this represents the frictional loss of attention, but ⅓ is an assumed value; in reality, it may be 1/300. Not everyone who hears the narrative will buy in. Not every investment will translate into long-term holding value. This coefficient reminds us that Meme's market capitalization can never fully cover the attention it receives; there is significant bubble evaporation and attention shifting. 4.2 Trajectory Analysis: From "Point" to "Cone" The upward trajectory of Meme coin typically follows a cone-shaped formation process: The narrative x is just born, nodes y are few, and funds z are just beginning to be injected. At this point, the volume is negligible. **Rooting Downwards:** As more nodes y join, the narrative x begins to fission. The most important aspect at this stage is not price (height), but increasing the number of holding addresses and community activity (expanding the radius r). **Vertical Eruption:** When the consensus base is large enough, the inflow of funds z creates a remarkable leverage effect. Since the base radius is already fixed, increasing the same amount of z will rapidly expand the volume V. **A Successful Meme Coin** The trajectory, in essence, is to first expand the base and then increase the height. 5. Boundaries and Limitations of the Cone Volume Formula 5.1 Defining the Growth States of "Healthy" and "Deformed" This model sets r (narrative × nodes) as a square term and z (funds) as a linear term. It has two main and significant effects: It captures network effects: In the meme field, a 10x increase in narrative spread (r) can often support 100 The market capitalization (V) increases by a factor of two. This explains why some coins, even if their capital (h) hasn't reached its breakout point, can experience exponential valuation jumps as long as their community foundation (r) is strong enough. This explains the physical structure of a "crash": If a project's radius r (consensus) is shrinking, even if you invest a lot of capital z to pump the price, the cone's volume will rapidly shrink due to the collapse of the bottom. Furthermore, through this geometric model, we can easily distinguish two typical failure models: "Telephone Pole" Model (High z, Extremely Low r): Only large investors engage in wash trading or Ponzi schemes to drive up the price, lacking community discussion and narrative resonance. This cone is extremely unstable; once the capital z... Withdrawal instantly reduces volume to zero. The "Big Pie" Model (Large r, Extremely Low z): The community is very lively, and the narrative is good, but there's no capital inflow. This cone-shaped structure has only area and no volume, representing a poor community that's "praised but not popular." 5.2 Limitations In real-world secondary market applications, the following "physical distortions" need attention: Coupling Relationships Between Variables: In the formula, r and h are independent variables, but in the Meme market, an increase in z (capital) will, in turn, strengthen x (narrative) and y (propagation nodes). This is the famous "price-driven" effect. The higher the price, the more the narrative resembles the truth, and the faster the nodes spread. Dynamics of the coefficient: In an extremely bullish market, this coefficient may approach 1 (becoming a cylinder) because emotions smooth out all losses; while in a bear market, this coefficient may become 1/1000, meaning that most attention cannot be converted into purchasing power. Missing time dimension: A cone is a static geometry, but a meme has a time limit. Introducing a "decay rate" would make the model more perfect, because the narrative of a meme will naturally shrink with aesthetic fatigue. 6. Case Study of Broccoli 6.1. Elements of Broccoli (Content/Narrative): "CZ personally endorses" – this is top-tier narrative and influence. (Dissemination Nodes): CZ's millions of followers, the official BNB Chain account, the Four.Meme platform, and the "scientists" and PVP players lurking across the internet. (Funds/Market Value): Existing funds on the BNB chain, and "hot money" from other chains. 6.2. Four-Stage Trajectory Reconstruction **Stage 1: Bottom (Start) – Fog Period** Before CZ's announcement on February 13, 2025, x, y, and z were almost zero, but the market was filled with "static electricity." The entire internet began speculating about the name, resulting in numerous tiny cones of incorrect direction, such as BNBDog and CZDoge. **Stage 2: Ignition – Narrative Explosion** CZ posted a tweet officially announcing the dog's name as Broccoli with a picture. The y-axis (node) instantly exploded. Twitter and Telegram channels spread almost simultaneously within seconds. Since x (narrative) was directly given by CZ, its authenticity is confirmed. Funds z A frenzy ensued, with hundreds or even thousands of tokens bearing the same name instantly generated on platforms like Four.Meme. The trajectory line at this moment exhibits a near-vertical y-z plane tilt. **Phase Three: Spin – Jungle Combat (PVP)** Within 2-12 hours of the name reveal, tens of thousands of Broccoli appeared on the market. To compete for the "real" identity, various communities began a frenzy of secondary creation (competition for x). At this point, the model is in a rotating state. Some tokens increase z through spontaneous community purchases, while others enrich x with more interesting Meme graphs. Only those Broccoli that can continuously stimulate y (more node discussion) and enrich x (narrative upgrades, such as doing charity or making games) can maintain rotation; the rest, due to a lack of x, lose their rotational force and directly enter the collapse process. Phase Four: Ascension – Consensus Consolidation Ultimately, only 1-2 "chosen ones" emerge, gaining the attention of the BNB Foundation or recognition from mainstream exchanges. At this point, the base area r = x * y has solidified due to long-term community building. The narrative has evolved from "CZ's dog" to "BNB Chain's mascot." Even with occasional fluctuations in funds z, the market capitalization cone remains standing due to the extremely stable foundation. 6.3 Warning: The Ascended Broccolis In the chaotic battle of 2025, 99.99% of Broccolis fell into an "inverted V-shaped" crash. During their initial launch phase, they artificially inflated the z-axis using deception or internal manipulation. Devs were purely focused on profiting, with no intention of cultivating x (content) or y (real community nodes). Therefore, this cone is an extremely thin "needle" that, after reaching its peak on the z-axis, instantly loses balance due to the base radius r approaching zero, causing its trajectory to plummet vertically, leaving a mess behind. CZ's Broccoli case proves that the ultimate battle for Meme coins is not about who issues their coin first (z), but about who can use the power of the community (y) to pave the "CZ's pet" point (x) into the largest possible circle (r). Broccoli should be the largest "same-base-area cone speed race" on Meme in 2025. Under the same x narrative, tens of thousands of projects are competing for y and... z ultimately proved that only "narrative upgrade + node stickiness" could achieve a breakthrough in market value.

      7. Case Analysis of Pnut

      Three-Dimensional Coordinate Decomposition of Pnut

      X-axis (Narrative Density - Emotion and Justice): This is not just a squirrel meme; it carries the narratives of "resisting the excessive expansion of public power" and "avenging innocent lives." From "a cute squirrel → a hero killed by the system → a political totem of the MAGA movement." Its narrative density has achieved a rare cross-border and cross-class resonance in the history of memes.

      Y-axis (Spreading Nodes - Dimensional Reduction Attack): Top Node Y_max: Elon Musk tweeted continuously, followed by Joe Top influencers like Rogan followed suit. These nodes are not simply "market-calling KOLs," but giant nodes with the ability to set social agendas.

      Z-axis (funding carrier - emotional leverage): The extremely fast liquidity on the Solana chain, coupled with the lightning-fast listing of top CEXs like Binance, caused funds z to flow in rapidly.

      Stage 1. Ignition Phase: The Gene Injection of Tragic Narrative

      x (justice/revenge) is formed instantly, y (local community) begins to spread.

      The trajectory bounces vertically from the origin. The New York Environmental Protection Agency's actions injected a highly penetrating x gene into Pnut. At this time, z (funds) is still sluggish on the chain, but the energy at the bottom has begun to accumulate.

      Phase 2. The hovering period: the "gravitational pull" of top nodes

      The Y-axis undergoes a magnitude jump due to Elon Musk's intervention (Y → Y_max).

      The surge in price z attracted the attention of the entire network. Due to the strong reflexivity of Elon's tweets, every time the price doubles, it attracts more traditional media and political KOLs (new nodes) to join the discussion. The trajectory line begins to rotate violently in three-dimensional space, with a radius r Rapid expansion.

      Stage 3. Dimensional Ascension: From "Squirrel" to "Political Totem"

      The X-axis completes its dimensional ascension (upgrading from "animal protection" to "US election/resistance to the system").

      This is Pnut's most solid step. Because of the upgrade of the narrative x, the base area of ​​the cone, r = x*y, is no longer limited to the crypto circle. At this stage, Pnut is no longer a mongrel, but a cultural asset. Even if the funds z experiences a phased correction, the huge consensus base can withstand the selling pressure.

      Stage 4. Value Black Hole: The Collapse and Reconstruction of Blue-Chip Consensus

      Listed on top exchanges such as Binance, funds Z reached its peak. A massive V (market capitalization) has formed. It began to absorb liquidity within the Solana ecosystem like a black hole. At this point, Pnut's cone had completely hardened, becoming the object of imitation for all subsequent animal-related memes. In conclusion, after reading so much theoretical analysis, you might ask me, as an ordinary user, how should I identify, participate in, and embrace meme opportunities on BNB Chain, Solana, Base, and Xlayer? How can I seize big opportunities and achieve great results? To find out what happens next, stay tuned for the next installment.

data.title: Electric Capital: 26 Opportunities in 2026
data.description:
Source: Electric Capital; Compiled by: Jinse Finance

Key Points

  • Globally, trust in institutions is collapsing.

    Key Points

    • Globally, trust in institutions is collapsing.

    • IV. Finance as Entertainment: The younger generation views the marketplace as entertainment. Transactions are fast, social, and fun. This is changing the landscape of financial products and giving rise to new markets.

    • V. The Metaverse Revival: World models and generative AI have significantly reduced the cost of building immersive, personalized environments. People will be immersed in experiences tailored to their needs, rather than passively consuming content. There is a huge opportunity to build platforms that simplify the world-creating process and empower users to control how their data is shared, stored, and monetized within these worlds.

    • VI. New Cryptographic Primitives and Applications: The maturation of Proof-of-Stake and Proof-of-Work mechanisms has opened up space for new consensus models. Zero-knowledge systems and fully homomorphic encryption are becoming feasible.

    • These primitives open up new design spaces: consensus related to human or physical input, infrastructure for default privacy, and applications built upon this foundation for regulated entities, energy markets, and even emerging jurisdictions.

    I. Personal Software

    For the first time, individual users are able to build software fully tailored to their own needs, no longer limited to services provided by enterprises. As AI agents can now handle complex workflows such as reading emails, scheduling meetings, and managing documents, the demand for data privacy, data ownership, and data persistence is growing. Encryption enables these tools to be private, persistent, and collaborative with multiple users.

    Specific projects we want to invest in:

    1. Private AI Agents: People need to run AI on sensitive data in a secure manner.

    It might look like: An AI assistant that automates your personal workflows while protecting your privacy. Connect to your health and financial records to gain AI insights. The AI ​​model runs in a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) or on a computing network, with all inbound queries anonymized.

I. Personal Software

For the first time, individual users can build software that is fully tailored to their own needs, no longer limited to services provided by enterprises.

When the response is returned, neither the enterprise provider nor malicious actors can see your data. 2. Encrypted Collaboration Space: People need to collaborate privately with others (including humans and intelligent agents). Remember, the "cloud" is just someone else's computer. It might look like: a shared workspace for friends, family, or small businesses. Finances, documents, and tasks are synchronized through peer-to-peer storage solutions. Selective disclosure allows agents to access specific data types. No account creation is required, no company reads, stores, or trains sensitive data, and it can be used offline. 3. Desktop Agent: People need to automate the processing of data on their local computers. It might look like: an intelligent agent program installed on your desktop running locally, responsible for reading your emails, replying to messages, creating schedules, and managing your life. In an AI-first era, this concept can be extended to a new type of desktop operating system. 4. Privacy-Defined Payment Services: People need ways to pay for software services without revealing their identity.

It might look like this: You can purchase VPN, gaming, cloud storage, or AI computing services without registering an account. Pay-as-you-go pricing is metered by the service provider and settled in stablecoins via x402 or similar protocols. The service provider knows that the user has paid and the amount paid, but not the user's identity.

II. Agent-Centric Infrastructure

Agents will write most of our code and do most of our knowledge work. Key impacts include: (1) Software tools will need to be rebuilt from scratch because AI-generated code will introduce new failure modes. (2) Development will shift in-house as custom software is now more cost-effective. (3) New channels of interaction will be needed between agents. (4) Enterprises that were once limited by manpower can suddenly scale. These perspectives capture the opportunities presented by these secondary effects.

Specific projects we want to invest in:

5. AI-Native Computing Infrastructure: Enterprises need to be able to test, isolate, and revert AI-generated changes at the infrastructure level.

It might look like: AWS or GCP rebuilt for intelligent agents. Agents write code in a sandbox, safely test with production data, and automatically roll back deployments if issues arise. The entire process assumes the code is written by an intelligent agent, not a human.

6. End-to-End Product Development Tools: Non-technical staff need to turn ideas into working software.

It might look like: A platform where users can specify business goals, data sources, and expected results. The system automatically generates plans, designs, code, and working products. This system requires no technical translation, enabling non-technical users to turn "ideas" into "deployed products" in hours (not months).

7. Intelligent Agent-Enabled Commerce: Intelligent agents need to buy and sell autonomously, without human identity or bank accounts.

It might look like: An API marketplace where agents can purchase services from other agents. Service discovery, negotiation, and pay-per-use all use protocols like x402 and are settled instantly in stablecoins.

8. Data Networks and Marketplaces: Artificial intelligence needs data infrastructure to compensate contributors and empower them with access. It could look like a network where users can share medical records, spending patterns, investment behavior, or creative work for AI training. Contributors can set permissions and be paid as their data improves models. AI companies can obtain the necessary and clearly sourced financial data. 9. Expanding Professional Services: Service businesses need AI-native operations to achieve scalability beyond human capabilities. It could look like a law firm where each lawyer has an AI assistant for research, drafting, and document review. A firm that once served 1,000 clients now serves 100,000. Any client service industry—lawyers, architects, marketers, accountants, financial advisors—can be reshaped with AI at its core.

III. Fintech and DeFi

Over 4 billion individuals and millions of businesses facing currency risk are actively seeking access to US dollars through stablecoins, representing the largest expansion of the dollar network effect in decades. As stablecoins provide global individuals with access to US dollars—growing from $3 billion in 2019 to over $300 billion today—millions of new dollar holders need more than just digital cash. They need yields, investment opportunities, and financial services. The space for financial products that empower users with ownership and global access holds increasing opportunities.

Specific projects we want to invest in:

10. Cryptocurrency-agnostic yields: Stablecoin holders need yields that don't decline with the price of Bitcoin.

It might look like: A platform that delivers real infrastructure yields to stablecoin holders. Yields could come from data center project bonds, solar installations, and electric vehicle charging networks—projects with predictable cash flows that are agnostic to cryptocurrencies.

11. Globally Accessible Equities: Global investors need direct access to overseas investment opportunities with low friction and low cost. It could look like: a financial product that simulates equity ownership, provides price exposure, has no financing rates, and no maturity date. A Filipino trader built a US technology portfolio. A Canadian trader built a South Korean semiconductor portfolio. 12. New Types of Insurance: Businesses need fast, transparent insurance to address operational risks that traditional insurance cannot provide. It could look like: a platform that uses prediction markets to create new insurance products. For example, a hotel chain could purchase hurricane insurance for its properties in Florida. A ski resort could hedge against the risk of a mild winter. Funding providers offer liquidity in exchange for market-independent returns. 13. On-Chain Commodity Markets: Commodities need markets that offer 24/7 trading, instant settlement, and global access. It could look like: an energy storage capacity trading market. Battery storage capacity is a potential entry point because data centers require a reliable power supply and are investing in energy storage to reduce their reliance on the grid and integrate renewable energy. Data centers with excess storage capacity can sell it to nearby facilities during peak electricity demand. Grid operators can trade capacity based on seasonal demand.

14. Protected DeFi Assets: Institutions need to deploy assets in DeFi to ensure their safety even in the event of a hack.

It might look like: a wrapped version of Ethereum that can be rolled back if the protocol is attacked (GuardedETH). A trusted committee reviews the vulnerability and can roll back GuardedETH without affecting the underlying Ethereum. Legitimate transactions continue as usual.

IV. Finance as Entertainment

The younger generation sees financial markets as an elitist alternative to traditional paths. They participate in the market, reimagine its form, and transform it into entertainment. They trade like they're playing a game: seeking quick feedback and exciting trades in markets that are easy to learn and participate in.

For example, fast-settlement products like zero-day options account for over 55% of S&P 500 options trading volume, with settlements completed within hours. Markets like prediction markets, where anyone can invest in news, are expected to reach $44 billion in trading volume by 2025, a fivefold increase from the previous year. They are also turning trading into content: discussing positions in real-time on Discord, sharing profits and losses on TikTok, and reviewing portfolios on Twitch. When markets become a form of entertainment, it creates opportunities for new platforms that treat financial data like users—as interesting and engaging content.

Specific projects we want to invest in:

15. Audience Participation Capital: Live viewers need ways to participate economically in the outcomes.

What it could look like: A platform that allows viewers to bet on live content. Participation makes the viewing experience more engaging, but currently viewers can only tip and subscribe. This platform could allow reality TV viewers to predict who will be eliminated, or to copy trades following the streamer's trading process.

16. Opinion Market: Prediction market platforms need markets based on collective beliefs rather than events. It might look like: a platform that generates market ranking lists. Users can bet on how they think others will rank these items. The platform would create lists such as the best pizza in New York, the best wine under $20, the most influential movie of the past decade, or the best AI development tool, all ranked by the market. The lists are adjusted weekly based on user betting weights. 17. TV Series Production Platform: Today, independent writers can produce TV series much cheaper than studios, so they need funding and distribution platforms. It might look like: a user-generated content (UGC) platform for short-form dramas. Creators use AI video tools to create series: gangster boyfriend saga, billionaire secrets revealed, revenge thrillers, and more. Fans can unlock series with tokens and directly tip creators. Creators' income depends on viewership. ReelShort generated over $700 million in revenue in the first quarter of 2025 with its low-cost, studio-produced series. The platform combines YouTube's user-generated content with ReelShort's video format.

V. The Metaverse Revival

Building immersive digital worlds is now economically viable. Over the past two years, AI models for images, videos, and simulations have advanced rapidly, significantly reducing the cost of creating assets and environments. Individual creators can now produce content that previously required an entire game studio. Simultaneously, the demand for personalized, interactive content is accelerating: Dispatch, an interactive TV/game hybrid that allows users to choose their own storyline, sold 3.3 million copies in just three months, generating $85 million in revenue with a 98% positive review rate. Roblox saw a 70% year-over-year increase in daily active users and paid creators $428 million in the third quarter of 2025 alone. Personalized, AI-driven character chat applications like Character AI are also showing strong early demand, meeting users' needs for personalized entertainment. These new environments not only entertain users but also generate rich, structured interactive data for world models and robotics. Specific projects we want to invest in: 18. World Compiler: Independent creators without specialized skills need tools that can translate natural language into fully interactive 3D environments. It might look like: A platform that translates natural language into fully interactive 3D worlds. Building 3D environments still requires specialized skills in modeling, physics, and NPC behavior. Artificial intelligence can break down this barrier. Creators only need to describe a world, and the system can build it. Resources, physics, NPC logic, and memory are all handled automatically by the system. Creators can deliver a rich virtual environment in just a few days, instead of years. 19. Procedural Narrative Engine: Players need stories that adapt to them and are endless. It might look like: A platform that can generate player-specific stories in real time. Linear stories have endings, but players want a game experience that adapts to their needs and continues to evolve. Users enter a detective world where each case is unique. Characters remember past interactions, plot twists change based on player choices, and the story never ends. 20. World as Dataset Platform: World models and robot systems require diverse interaction data. Immersive consumer environments constantly generate this data, but currently no one is collecting it. It might look like this: A virtual reality game where every player interaction is recorded. How users move around the room, pick up items, and interact with characters becomes data for robot training. Users can choose to join, set data-sharing permissions, and receive corresponding rewards. Artificial intelligence companies can obtain real human behavior data that they cannot generate synthetically. VI. New Cryptographic Primitives and Applications Cryptographic primitives are no longer just theoretical. Proof-of-Stake (PoS) and Proof-of-Work (PoW) have proven their robustness in large-scale applications. Zero-knowledge proofs (ZK) are moving from the research stage to production systems. Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) is becoming increasingly faster and more available. As these foundational technologies mature, they create new opportunities for developers to build systems that prioritize privacy, embed real-world inputs into consensus mechanisms, and support coordination with traditional systems such as energy markets or governments. Specific projects we want to invest in: 21. Human-Time-Based Consensus: Blockchain networks need consensus mechanisms based on human effort, not just capital. It might look like this: Based on practical proof-of-work, consensus is achieved by completing tasks with external value, such as labeling data or verifying real-world events. Participation rights stem from demonstrated capabilities, not vested interests. 22. Physical Resource Networks: Small infrastructure operators need to coordinate systems to ensure their contributions are economically justified. It might look like this: In energy networks, the weights of production or storage are determined by consensus, thus aligning grid stability with network security. Sensor networks are closely integrated with physical measurement data such as weather, water quality, or infrastructure monitoring. 23. Privacy-Native L1: Healthcare, businesses, regulated finance, and many other industries require supply chains with privacy as the default setting. It might look like this: By default, computation is performed on encrypted data, forming a confidential state machine. Current blockchains are transparent by default, but many institutions, such as healthcare, businesses, and regulated financial institutions, cannot legally operate on transparent blockchains. Validators use a native zero-knowledge proof architecture or a fully homomorphic encryption-based execution method to verify transactions without viewing the transaction content. 24. FHE for Specific Use Cases: Institutions often need to collaborate on data processing without revealing their data to each other. It might look like this: Banks detect suspicious patterns between institutions without sharing customer data. Each bank runs fully homomorphic queries (FHE queries) on encrypted data from other banks. They identify accounts that have been in contact with the same suspicious entity without revealing their customer lists to each other. 25. Energy Contract Settlement: Traditional markets need cryptocurrency payment channels to enable 24/7 settlement between different participants. Unregulated energy markets are a good entry point because they are outdated and increasingly pressured by the growing energy demand driven by artificial intelligence. It could look like: A shared settlement layer for energy contracts in unregulated markets. Settlement data triggers automatic payments. Suppliers view cash flow in real time. Brokers receive commissions instantly. No single party controls the ledger. 26. Crypto-Native Jurisdictions: Special economic zones and frontier regions need new ways of thinking about governance and financial infrastructure. It could look like: A completely new jurisdiction that adopts crypto infrastructure from the outset. On-chain identity, programmable courts, tokenized capital markets, and regulatory logic based on smart contracts.

data.title: Delphi Digital: 2026 is the year of Solana.
data.description:
Author: Delphi Digital; Source: X, @Delphi_Digital; Compiled by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Solana's 2026 roadmap may be the most radical upgrade cycle in the network's history, completely transforming everything from consensus mechanisms to infrastructure into a decentralized Nasdaq. Solana's roadmap aims to transform it into an exchange-grade environment, enabling its native on-chain Centralized Limit Order Book (CLOB) to rival centralized exchanges (CEXs) in terms of latency, liquidity depth, and fairness. The following are all the upgrades that achieve this goal. Alpenglow: Consensus Architecture Reform Alpenglow represents the most significant protocol-level change in Solana's history. It introduces a completely new consensus architecture built around two components: Votor and Rotor. Votor has revolutionized the way the network reaches consensus. Instead of chaining multiple rounds of voting together, validators aggregate the voting results off-chain and complete the final confirmation within one or two rounds. Theoretically, the final confirmation time can be reduced to 100-150 milliseconds, far lower than the previous 12.8 seconds. Votor runs two block confirmation paths in parallel. If a block receives overwhelming support (more than 80% of the stake) in the first round, it is confirmed immediately. If the support rate is between 60% and 80%, a second round begins. If the support rate in the second round also exceeds 60%, the block is confirmed. This design ensures that the block can be eventually confirmed even if some nodes in the network are unresponsive. Rotor revolutionized the way blocks are propagated, routing messages directly through high-stakes validators with reliable bandwidth.

Alpenglow also introduces a "20+20" resilience model: security is guaranteed as long as malicious stake does not exceed 20% of the total stake; even if another 20% of the stake is offline, transaction finality is still guaranteed. This means that even if up to 40% of the stake in the network is in a malicious or inactive state, Alpenglow can maintain transaction finality.

In the Alpenglow architecture, the proof-of-history mechanism has actually been abandoned, replaced by deterministic time slot scheduling and local timers.

**This upgrade is expected to launch between early and mid-2026.** Firedancer: Runtime Improvements **Since its inception, Solana has relied on a single validator client (now called Agave). This monolithic approach has long been one of the network's core weaknesses. Any vulnerability or failure at the client level could cripple the entire network.** Firedancer is the second standalone validator client developed by Jump, written in C++. Its design goal is to transform Solana's validator into a deterministic, high-throughput engine capable of handling millions of TPS with minimal latency fluctuations. Frankendancer, as a transitional solution, combines Firedancer's network and block generation modules with Agave's runtime and consensus components. As Firedancer gradually moves towards mainnet readiness, validator diversity should increase significantly. Against this competitive backdrop, both teams have undertaken numerous iterative updates. DoubleZero: High-Performance Fiber Infrastructure DoubleZero is a private network overlay that connects validator nodes via dedicated fiber optic cables, similar to the microsecond-level transmission infrastructure relied upon by traditional exchanges like Nasdaq and the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. As the set of validator nodes grows, message propagation becomes more challenging. The more nodes there are, the more targets there are, which introduces time inconsistencies in the network. DoubleZero eliminates this inconsistency by transmitting messages through optimal paths, rather than having messages repeatedly hop across the public internet. Alpenglow's finality model relies on validators receiving and responding to messages within strict time windows. If propagation is inconsistent, votes arrive late, the formation of a quorum slows down, and the time required for final confirmation is extended. DoubleZero reduces the latency gap between validators, enabling Votors to complete final confirmation faster and Rotors to propagate more evenly. DoubleZero also supports multicast, which replicates data within the network and delivers it to validators simultaneously. Block Building: BAM and Harmonic Two complementary trends are reshaping Solana's block building layer: BAM (Block Assembly Marketplace) is Jito's reimagining of the Solana transaction pipeline. Unlike letting slot leaders unilaterally determine the order, BAM inserts a market and privacy layer that separates ordering from execution. Transactions are imported into a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE), meaning that validators and builders cannot see the original transaction content until the ordering takes effect. This prevents speculative pre-execution behavior such as front-running. Harmonic focuses on another aspect of the process: who builds the blocks. It introduces an open block builder aggregation layer, allowing validators to accept block proposals from multiple competing builders in real time. Harmonic can be understood as a meta-market, while BAM can be understood as a micro-market. Raiku: Deterministic Execution Guarantees. Raiku fills the remaining gap. While Solana solves most throughput bottlenecks, it doesn't inherently provide deterministic latency or programmable execution guarantees for specific applications. High-frequency trading and on-chain CLOBs require finer-grained control than L1 caching can provide.

Raiku provides a scheduling/auction layer that runs in parallel with Solana's set of validator nodes, providing applications with a programmable, deterministic pre-execution environment without modifying L1 consensus. It guarantees execution through advance-to-the-money (AOT) transactions and just-in-time (JIT) transactions for pre-submitted workflows to meet the demands of real-time execution. Bringing Capital Markets On-Chain Solana still dominates the hyperscale blockchain space, but this dominance is meaningless without users and an efficient on-chain market. While the vast majority of Meme coins are still traded on Solana, the on-chain perpetual contract trading market is rapidly consolidating into a few exchanges. To compete with centralized platforms, performance must be on par. We believe the Solana ecosystem is aware of this and remains optimistic about closing the gap. Upgrades in the pipeline are promising, and we expect new Solana-native perpetual contracts like BULK to launch early next year. Furthermore, retail demand for trading spot assets on Solana remains strong. While Hyperliquid currently dominates the perpetual contract market, Solana has become the preferred L1 network for trading any spot pair. While centralized exchanges (CEXs) still hold a commanding lead, Solana remains the preferred on-chain trading platform. Products like xStocks are bringing on-chain equities directly to Solana. Liquidity, price discovery, and speculative attention are concentrating on a single chain that offers faster settlement, a better user experience, and more concentrated capital. This is the reason Solana is bringing capital markets on-chain.

data.title: BTC falls below $88,000: Is the "Sell-Off in the US" trade impacting the crypto market?
data.description:
Shaw, Jinse Finance

Affected by the global macroeconomic market turmoil, Bitcoin fell rapidly again, briefly dipping below $88,000, reaching a low of $87,895.98, a drop of over 5.3% in 24 hours, before recovering somewhat. Ethereum followed the market trend, briefly dipping below $3,000, reaching a low of $2,920, a drop of over 7% in 24 hours.

Coinglass data shows that in the past 24 hours, a total of $1.068 billion in positions were liquidated across the network, including $1 billion in long positions and $67.9526 million in short positions, with long positions being the primary focus. BTC liquidations totaled $439 million, and ETH liquidations totaled $393 million.

As geopolitical tensions in Greenland escalate and domestic fiscal concerns in Japan trigger a global bond market crash, the "sell-off in America" ​​trade has returned to the market spotlight with a vengeance. US stocks, bonds, and currencies all fell sharply, Japanese long-term bonds plummeted, and spot gold hit a new high. The Bitcoin and crypto markets, along with major asset markets, experienced simultaneous volatility. Have macroeconomic factors become another "black swan" event affecting cryptocurrencies? Has the "sell-off in America" ​​trade also impacted the crypto market? I. Crypto Market and Macro Markets Experience Simultaneous Volatility, Erasing Most of the Year-End Gains. Starting last night, Bitcoin and major crypto markets continued their downward trend. This morning, Bitcoin experienced another rapid decline, briefly falling below $88,000 and touching $87,895.98, a drop of over 5.3% in 24 hours, almost erasing most of its gains since the beginning of the year, before recovering slightly. Ethereum followed the market trend, briefly falling below $3,000 and touching $2,920, a drop of over 7% in 24 hours. According to Coinglass data, in the past 24 hours, a total of $1.068 billion in positions were liquidated across the entire network, including $1 billion in long positions and $67.9526 million in short positions, with long positions being the primary target. Of these, $439 million was liquidated in BTC and $393 million in ETH. In the past 24 hours, over 181,000 people worldwide have been liquidated, with the largest single liquidation occurring on Bitget - BTCUSDT_UMCBL worth $13.52 million. Meanwhile, the Japanese long-term government bond market experienced a "frenzied" sell-off, while Trump claimed that Greenland was essential and threatened to impose tariffs on several European countries ahead of the Davos high-level meeting. The market saw a resurgence of the "sell-off America" ​​trading pattern, with US stocks, US bonds, and the US dollar all declining throughout the day. Safe-haven flows pushed gold to new highs, rising nearly 2% intraday. The Japanese bond crisis combined with the Greenland crisis triggered a major shock to global macroeconomic markets. Consequently, the decline in Bitcoin and the cryptocurrency market is not surprising. Currently, the cryptocurrency market will continue to be affected by changes in global macroeconomic policies and industry regulations in the short term. II. The Greenland Crisis Between the US and Europe Continues to Escalate, with No Signs of Resolution. According to CCTV News, US President Trump stated that his goal of controlling Greenland "will never change" and refused to rule out the possibility of seizing Greenland by force. Trump clarified his objective on social media: to seize sovereignty over Greenland from NATO ally Denmark. In an interview, Trump declined to comment on whether he would use force to seize Greenland, stating "no comment." Meanwhile, the European Parliament announced a freeze on the approval process for the trade agreement reached with the US last July. This is seen as the EU's first response to the latest pressure from US President Trump. Affected by the escalating US-EU dispute, the Danish pension fund Akademiker Pension will exit the US Treasury market, planning to sell its holdings of US Treasury bonds by the end of this month. As of the end of December, the pension fund held $100 million in U.S. Treasury bonds. The fund's chief investment officer stated that the U.S. is "basically no longer a good credit destination." However, U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessenter told reporters at the Davos Economic Forum in Switzerland on Tuesday that claims of a European sell-off of U.S. Treasury bonds were "completely illogical," and that there was "no such discussion" in Europe. He called it a "false narrative" and expressed "strong opposition" to it. This statement aimed to stabilize market confidence in the prospects for U.S. Treasury demand, especially given the close scrutiny of the movements of major global creditors. He also stated that Europe is a center of regulatory chaos and urged all countries to adhere to trade agreements. Trump's remarks and market concerns about a European sell-off of U.S. Treasury bonds triggered a "sell-off in America" ​​trading pattern, with U.S. stocks, bonds, and the dollar all declining during the day. The S&P 500 fell 2.1% in a single day, wiping out its year-to-date gains, and large-cap tech stocks fell to their lowest point since November of last year. The US stock market lost over $1.3 trillion in market capitalization in a single day. The US dollar index briefly fell below the 98 mark. Gold's status as a safe-haven asset was once again highlighted. Against the backdrop of a weakening dollar, gold prices were pushed up by aggressive buying, with spot gold breaking through $4,800 to reach a new high, accumulating a gain of over 10% this month. US long-term bonds were sold off, with the 10-year Treasury yield climbing to 4.293% and the 2-year yield rising by 1.89 basis points. The correlation between the crypto market and macro asset markets such as US stocks is increasingly strong, and this synchronized sharp drop with major asset markets is a significant manifestation of this correlation. III. Japan's economic policy environment triggered a sharp drop in Japanese bonds, igniting market concerns. On January 20, the Japanese bond market suddenly experienced a sharp sell-off, described by traders as "the most chaotic trading day in recent years." The sell-off, which started slowly, quickly escalated into a full-blown shock, pushing yields on some long-term bonds to record highs. The core of the market panic was Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's tax cut and spending increase plan, which triggered deep concerns among investors about the sustainability of Japan's fiscal policy. Japan is one of the world's most indebted governments. According to Xinhua News Agency, Prime Minister Takaichi stated at a press conference on January 19th that she would dissolve the House of Representatives on January 23rd and seek voter authorization to continue governing, holding a House of Representatives election on February 8th. The current term of the members of the House of Representatives was originally scheduled to expire in October 2028. Takaichi stated bluntly at the press conference: "I will end the excessively tight fiscal policy... We must break free from the shackles of excessive tightening and boldly invest in risk management." Market analysts pointed out that the market is pricing in a Japanese version of the "Trath moment." The term "Trath moment" originates from former British Prime Minister Lids Truss. In 2022, her introduction of a series of tax cuts lacking funding triggered a sharp sell-off in the UK bond market, ultimately leading to her swift resignation. The yields on Japanese 30-year and 40-year government bonds surged by more than 25 basis points in a single day, marking the largest single-day fluctuation since the impact of Trump's tariffs on global markets last year. Simultaneously, weak 20-year bond auction results exacerbated concerns about Sanae Takashi's fiscal policy, creating a vicious cycle of "sell-up-intensified concerns-further sell-up." The growing bearish sentiment towards Japanese government bonds among global bond investors quickly spread to the global long-term bond market, contributing to the emergence of "selling US" trades. IV. Market Concerns Persist Regarding the Independence of the Federal Reserve U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant stated in Davos on Tuesday that President Trump "may announce his nominee for the new Federal Reserve Chairman as early as next week," and that there are currently "four excellent candidates." Furthermore, according to the Associated Press, Federal Reserve Chairman Powell will attend oral arguments before the U.S. Supreme Court on Wednesday regarding Federal Reserve Governor Cook, marking an unusual public statement and show of support from the head of the U.S. central bank. The Supreme Court is hearing whether President Trump has the right to dismiss Governor Cook, appointed by former President Biden. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that Powell plans to attend Wednesday's hearing. This is also Powell's most public and direct support for Cook to date. The CEO of Citigroup stated that the independence of the Federal Reserve is crucial for the U.S. financial system to maintain its leading position globally. Analysts say that if Trump appoints a Federal Reserve chairman who is seen as overly compliant, the bond market will quickly "discipline" the US, while precious metals will remain a good tool for hedging against political volatility. The new chairman will take over a central bank grappling with a tug-of-war between institutional independence and administrative demands. Over the past year, current chairman Powell has been repeatedly criticized by Trump for the pace of interest rate cuts, and the market is concerned that the new chairman's political leanings will trigger bond market volatility and affect the dollar's trajectory, contributing to the so-called "sell-off in America" ​​trade. V. Market Analysis and Interpretation The "sell-off in America" ​​trade has manifested in multiple global macro markets, including the cryptocurrency market. What will happen next? Will this phenomenon continue? What impact will it have on the future trend of the cryptocurrency market? Let's look at some analyses and interpretations. 1. Citigroup's Global Markets division noted that the sharp rise in volatility in Japanese government bonds "could lead to increased volatility in other asset classes, particularly U.S. Treasuries, thus necessitating a reduction in overall portfolio size." Mohammed Apabhai, Citigroup's head of trading strategy for Asia, wrote in a report to clients on Tuesday that risk parity funds may need to sell up to one-third of their current risk exposure, potentially triggering a bond sell-off of up to $130 billion in the U.S. alone. 2. JPMorgan Asset Management's Chief Investment Officer, Bob Mitchell, stated that the market sell-off is a signal to the Trump administration to take action to restore calm, as officials did after the "Liberation Day tariffs" unsettled investors last year. "There's some turmoil in the market right now, and there's definitely some panic," Mitchell said. “The market experienced significant volatility in April, after which many things calmed down, and the market returned to normal. We need to hear something similar.” On Tuesday, the S&P 500 erased its year-to-date gains, and bonds and the dollar also fell. Fundstrat Research Director Tom Lee warned investors that the cryptocurrency and stock markets could experience a “painful decline” in 2026 due to geopolitical tensions, but a rebound is expected by the end of the year. In an interview on Tuesday, Lee said that 2026 will be similar to 2025, with the blockchain and AI sectors still benefiting, but the risks of tariffs and political divisions will initially limit sustained market gains. Lee expects a 15% to 20% correction in the stock market this year, but he said, “I think we’ll have a strong finish to the year,” partly due to the Fed’s dovish stance and the end of last year’s quantitative tightening. He also mentioned that the White House’s selection of “winners and losers” could influence the performance of various sectors this year. Regarding Bitcoin, Lee stated that he still expects it to reach a new all-time high this year, but did not mention the $250,000 price prediction he made several months ago. 4. David Picton, CEO of hedge fund Picton Investments, stated that if Trump appoints a Federal Reserve chairman perceived as overly compliant, the bond market will quickly "discipline" the US; precious metals remain a good tool for hedging against political volatility. "There is a correlation between the number of posts on Truth Social and the movement of so-called 'debasement trades' in the market—that is, gold, silver, and these commodity-based hedges," said David Picton. He was referring to Trump's preferred social media platforms. 5. Pepperstone analyst Michael Brown stated that Trump's new tariff threats against several European countries mark a sharp escalation in his actions to acquire Greenland. Market reaction was largely in line with expectations, with stock index futures falling across the board, the dollar weakening in a mild "Sell America" ​​trade, and precious metal prices surging. Despite numerous uncertainties and the need to closely monitor European reactions, Brown believes this is another of Trump's old tricks: first issue a dramatic threat, then escalate it significantly, forcing concessions and ultimately reaching some kind of agreement. For the market, this could mean some short-term volatility as news noise becomes deafening, but a rebound is expected when another "TACO" moment arrives. Brown believes this further solidifies the strong bullish case for precious metals. Equity fundamentals remain solid, providing opportunities to buy on dips. 6. Some analysts say that despite a sharp pullback in Bitcoin on Monday, durable ETF inflows offset short-term volatility, making the long-term price outlook for Bitcoin technically constructive. According to CoinGecke data, the cryptocurrency has stabilized around $92,000 and has changed little in the past 24 hours. This stabilization occurred after Monday's sell-off, which saw over $865 million in liquidations due to escalating US-EU trade tensions. Digital asset investment firm ZeroCap released a report on Tuesday stating, "The market recovered relatively quickly, with Bitcoin stabilizing in this range, showing strong bottom support, and most of the macro 'noise' has already been priced in." The firm's analysts likened the current market pattern to an "early risk appetite rotation," pointing to strong structural inflows from spot Bitcoin ETFs, which are more persistent than short-term positions. Despite last week's net ETF inflows reaching their highest level in three months, other analysts remain cautious. Sean Dawson, head of research at on-chain options platform Derive, also expressed caution. Analysts at Santiment noted that over the past nine days, Bitcoin whales and shark addresses have continuously increased their holdings by 36,322 Bitcoins, while retail investors have been selling, suggesting the crypto market may have the "best conditions" for a breakout. Santiment posted on the X platform that "Bitcoin whales and shark addresses are still accumulating," at which time Bitcoin had fallen 4.55% in 24 hours, with a price of $89,110 at the time of the post.

8. On-chain data analyst Murphy analyzes that Bitcoin investor sentiment is shifting from the "hesitation zone" to the "disappointment zone." Murphy points out that Bitcoin price cycles typically go through four emotional phases: the optimism zone, the profit-taking zone, the hesitancy zone, and the disappointment zone. Current market indicators show that investor sentiment is transitioning from the third phase (the hesitancy zone) to the fourth phase (the disappointment zone). Analysts say that between October and December 2025, whale groups holding 100-1000 Bitcoins actively increased their holdings, followed by whale groups holding 1000-10000 Bitcoins. However, large whales have now stopped accumulating, and some have even turned to selling, which is a sign of insufficient investor confidence. Analysts believe that the market needs a prolonged period of recovery or lower prices to attract demand in order to build a new consensus on market bottom.

data.title: Bitwise: Why We Are Bullish on Chainlink
data.description:
Author: Matt Hogan, Chief Investment Officer, Bitwise; Translated by Jinse Finance

I have some thoughts on this most undervalued, yet most important and attractive, crypto asset.

Last week, we launched a Chainlink exchange-traded product (ETP). By cryptocurrency industry standards, the launch was rather low-key: trading volume was decent, bid-ask spreads were stable, but it didn't generate the same frenzy as Bitcoin ETPs.

Investors are missing out. Chainlink is one of my most favored crypto assets. In fact, I believe it is currently the most misunderstood, yet most important, and likely severely undervalued crypto asset on the market.

In this week's memo, I want to share the reasons behind this. What is Chainlink? Chainlink is the 11th largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization globally, approaching $10 billion. However, investors rarely mention it. I believe this is mainly due to its overly complex business model. Most high-profile crypto assets have a simple and easy-to-understand positioning: Bitcoin is "digital gold," Ethereum is a platform for stablecoins and tokenized assets, and Dogecoin is a "meme coin." As for Chainlink… if you ask most people in the crypto world what it is, they will tell you, **it's a "data oracle" service provider.** But this explanation has two problems. First, it's incomplete. As I'll explain later, acting as an oracle is just one of Chainlink's businesses. As Zach Rynes recently stated in Bitwise's X Platform Space livestream, calling Chainlink a "data oracle" is like defining Amazon as "a bookstore." But the more crucial issue is that the general public has no idea what an "oracle" actually means. In the crypto space, most people consider oracles to be a service that allows blockchains to access real-world information. When a blockchain needs to query the price of short-term U.S. Treasury bonds, it uses oracles to obtain data; decentralized finance (DeFi) applications need to predict stock price movements; banks need to verify whether customers have passed Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks; and prediction market platforms like Polymarket need to verify whether the Senate has passed the Transparency Act—all these rely on oracles. The term "oracle" originates from ancient Greece. People would travel to temples to ask priests and priestesses for answers to worldly questions (such as the famous Oracle of Delphi), and these clergy would provide explanations. So, it seems ingenious to think of Chainlink as a blockchain "oracle," right? Actually, no. Very few people understand ancient Greek oracles, and to understand "decentralized oracles," most people probably need another "oracle" to explain it. My understanding of Chainlink is much simpler—I see it as a software platform company. Specifically, it is a high-growth software platform that holds a dominant share in a large and rapidly growing market. The core value of a software platform lies in problem-solving. Take Salesforce as an example; its role is to help people integrate and manage complex customer data. The core pain point that Chainlink aims to address is that blockchains are isolated from each other and cut off from the external real world. Currently, financial services and capital markets are accelerating their migration to blockchain, and Chainlink is a key support for this process. We can draw an analogy like this: blockchain is like an Excel spreadsheet installed on a new computer, not yet connected to the internet. This spreadsheet is powerful, capable of performing addition and subtraction, data sorting, calculus calculations, and even enabling cell-to-cell linkage through conditional statements. But the key problem is—it is in a completely isolated state. For this table to function effectively in the real world, especially in financial services, external connections are essential: It needs to acquire information such as stock prices and news events (this falls under the scope of Chainlink's "data oracle" service); it needs to interoperate with other tables (corresponding to Chainlink's "cross-chain interoperability" service); it needs to be compliant and meet regulatory requirements (i.e., Chainlink's "automated compliance" service); and it needs to orchestrate these functions into repeatable workflows via macros (i.e., Chainlink's "runtime environment" developer platform), and so on. Chainlink is the platform upon which almost all blockchains rely to achieve these functions. In the areas of infrastructure services connecting blockchains, crypto applications, and the real world, as well as between different blockchains, its market share ranges from 50% to 100%. The Core Logic of Investing in Chainlink: I firmly believe that institutional investor demand for Chainlink ETPs will eventually surge. The reason is simple: it is the key link connecting the various crypto ecosystems favored by institutional investors. Bullish on stablecoins? Stablecoin price supply, cross-chain transfers, and proof-of-reserve all rely on Chainlink's support. Bullish on asset tokenization? Pricing, asset servicing, and compliance in the tokenization process all depend on Chainlink. Bullish on decentralized finance (DeFi)? Without Chainlink, there would be no DeFi ecosystem as we know it today. Bullish on prediction markets? The answer is obvious—it's still Chainlink.

Similar examples abound.

A core trend in the crypto industry over the next decade will be its deep integration with the real world. Currently, the vast majority of connections between the crypto world and the real world are made through Chainlink. This is why numerous institutions and platforms, including SWIFT, Deutsche Börse, Euroclear, Fidelity Investments, Franklin Templeton Fund, FTSE Russell, Coinbase, decentralized lending platform Aave, JPMorgan Chase, BNP Paribas, Visa, Mastercard, the Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation of the United States (DTCC), and prediction market platform Polymarket, have chosen to use Chainlink. If you, like me, believe that a massive amount of stocks, bonds, and other assets will be migrated to the blockchain in the coming years, then you'll find that behind this transformation, a low-key yet powerful force is quietly driving everything—this is precisely why I firmly believe in Chainlink.

data.title: CFTC plans to upgrade crypto regulation; Trump Organization announces token registration date.
data.description:
Headline

▌CFTC Chairman Michael Selig Launches Future Proof Program to Upgrade Crypto Regulation

Michael Selig, Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), stated that the agency is developing a new roadmap and announced the launch of a new program called "Future Proof," aimed at modernizing the way digital assets are regulated. Selig stated that derivatives regulators need to upgrade and plans to conduct a broad review of existing rules, aiming to adopt the minimum effective dose of regulation he describes.
